message:
chore: sync multi-agent workspace updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -67,7 +67,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-11ck","title":"Demo Showcase: Action Timeline / Event Stream Viewer","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI is intentionally "kernel-like": deterministic rendering, explicit diffs, and a disciplined runtime loop. That makes it uniquely suited to *teach itself*.

This screen is an observability + pedagogy tool:
- show what the runtime is doing (messages, commands, subscriptions)
- show what the renderer is doing (diff size, ANSI bytes, frame timings)
- make fallbacks visible (capabilities, degradation decisions)

Done right, this becomes a jaw-dropping "X-ray view" of the engine.

## UX / Core Interactions

- Live scrollable timeline of events.
  - newest at bottom (with follow mode)
  - pause/follow toggle
- Filter bar:
  - by component (core/runtime/render/widgets)
  - by severity
  - by event type (input, cmd, subscription, render, present)
- Detail pane:
  - selecting an event shows structured fields and (when available) the reasoning ledger.

Suggested keybindings:
- F: toggle follow
- /: filter/search events
- Enter: toggle expanded detail
- Ctrl+T: theme cycle (coherent with the rest of the showcase)

## Architecture Notes

- Event source:
  - Integrate with ftui-runtime and/or tracing spans.
  - Prefer structured events (typed) over ad-hoc strings.

- Storage:
  - Keep an in-memory ring buffer for recent events.
  - Optional: record to JSONL for replay in bd-3sa7 (time travel).

- Rendering:
  - Use theme::intent/status styles for severity.
  - Keep allocations bounded (ring buffer + virtualization).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Timeline updates live during normal demo usage (inputs, commands, renders).
- Filtering is responsive and deterministic.
- Selecting an event shows structured fields (not just a formatted string).
- Provides at least one "galaxy brain" style card for a non-trivial decision (e.g., a fallback), showing:
  - the rule/evidence
  - the chosen action
  - a one-line intuition

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Sketch-based aggregation (bd-11ck.7):
  - summarize high-volume event types without losing correctness for the full stream
  - define error budgets and surface them in the UI
- Change-point alerts:
  - detect regime shifts (frame time spikes, diff byte spikes) and annotate the timeline
- Threshold calibration (bd-1rzr):
  - use conformal/e-process methods so alerts remain valid under optional stopping

## Extreme Optimization

- Keep the timeline view overhead small:
  - event ingestion should be O(1)
  - rendering should be virtualized and incremental
  - budgets measured and enforced (bd-11ck.2)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:55.022466235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:56:38.379146906Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["debug","demo","performance","ux"],"comments":[{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-11ck","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Display runtime events/actions in a timeline view.
- Filter by type and severity.

Testing
- Unit tests for event formatting and filtering.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs for event stream updates.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:55Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-11ck.6","title":"Action Timeline / Event Stream Viewer — UX/A11y Review","description":"UX/a11y review for Action Timeline / Event Stream Viewer: keybindings, focus order, and contrast/legibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Action Timeline / Event Stream Viewer — UX/A11y Review
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Visual rigor: enforce contrast bounds + deterministic rendering under fixed seeds; include snapshot fixtures and log theme IDs
- If any palette/gradient math: specify the color space + constraints (e.g., OKLCH) and prove WCAG targets in tests
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)
